suggested_next_actions: Remove the 2-second unlocked fallback in `acquire_state_lock()`; if the lock cannot be acquired, wait longer than the full Codex round trip or fail closed without mutating state.
Add a regression test that simulates overlapping Stop hooks and verifies the second invocation does not proceed until the first releases the lock, with the cooldown decrement preserved exactly once.

findings.title: Lock contention still falls through unlocked after ~2s
findings.explanation: This commit holds the state lock across the Codex call, but `acquire_state_lock()` still gives up after `LOCK_RETRY_MAX * LOCK_RETRY_SLEEP` (about 2 seconds) and returns `None`. A normal overlapping Stop hook will therefore stop waiting while the first hook is still inside the Codex round trip, then continue mutating state without any lock. That reintroduces the lost-update/cooldown race this change is meant to eliminate. The fix needs to block for the whole critical section or fail closed instead of proceeding unlocked. The mirrored template file has the same issue.
findings.recommendation: Do not return `None` and continue on timeout. Either wait until the lock is acquired, or skip all state mutation/output when contention is detected. Make the wait budget exceed the full Codex call duration if this path must serialize updates.

findings.title: No regression test covers the new locking semantics
findings.explanation: The bugfix changes inter-process coordination and cooldown persistence, but the diff does not add a test that forces two overlapping Stop-hook executions or a slow Codex call. Because the new behavior is concurrency-sensitive, a regression test is needed to prove the cooldown decrement survives contention and that the second call never falls back to unlocked writes.
findings.recommendation: Add a regression test that mocks a slow `analyze_with_codex()` call, starts a second invocation while the lock is held, and asserts the second path blocks or aborts safely and the state update happens once.

verdict.rationale: The intended race fix is undermined by the existing 2-second timeout fallback to unlocked state mutation, so overlapping hooks can still clobber cooldown/state. There is also no regression test for this concurrency path.
